meta.title: Bhagavad Gita — Ekam Sat Node Taxonomy
meta.description: 35 doctrinal nodes extracted from BG. Each node is a precise proposition, not a topic. These are the search targets for the MBH echo project.
meta.hk_note: Terms given in Harvard-Kyoto for grep against BORI corpus

nodes.name: Indestructibility of the Self
nodes.proposition: The ātman is unborn, eternal, undying — it cannot be killed by any weapon
nodes.notes: The bedrock of Krishna's entire argument. Everything else rests on this.

nodes.name: Self vs Body Distinction
nodes.proposition: The self (dehin) is distinct from and inhabits the body (deha); grief arises from confusing them
nodes.notes: The diagnostic of ignorance — mistaking the perishable for the eternal

nodes.name: Nishkama Karma — Action without Fruit-Attachment
nodes.proposition: One must act; the fruit of action is not one's right; do not make fruit the motive; do not embrace inaction
nodes.notes: BG 2.47 is the single most cited verse in entire tradition. Track its echoes across MBH.

nodes.name: Equanimity — Sama in Pleasure and Pain
nodes.proposition: The stable-minded person is unmoved by pleasure or pain, heat or cold, honor or dishonor
nodes.notes: Sthitaprajña description in 2.54-72 is a complete portrait — search for these qualities across MBH

nodes.name: The Sthitaprajna — Portrait of the Realized
nodes.proposition: The wise one whose mind is unmoved in sorrow, who does not long for pleasure, free of passion fear anger
nodes.notes: This is a CHARACTER description — check if any MBH sage is described in these terms

nodes.name: Three Gunas — Sattva Rajas Tamas
nodes.proposition: All of prakriti operates through three qualities — sattva (clarity), rajas (activity), tamas (inertia)
nodes.notes: Guna framework appears in Sankhya — track its use across all parvas

nodes.name: Field and Knower of the Field
nodes.proposition: The body is the field (kshetra); the self is the knower of the field (kshetrajna); Brahman knows all fields
nodes.notes: Chapter 13 framework — track ksetra/ksetrajna across MBH

nodes.name: Brahman Pervades All
nodes.proposition: Brahman is the substratum of all existence — in the smallest and the greatest
nodes.notes: The ekam sat node par excellence — one in all, all in one

nodes.name: Self is the Same in All Beings
nodes.proposition: One who sees the same Self in all beings, and all beings in the Self, does not hate
nodes.notes: THIS is the direct ekam sat node — oneness of self across all beings

nodes.name: Renunciation vs Abandonment — Tyaga vs Sannyasa
nodes.proposition: True renunciation is not abandonment of action but abandonment of attachment to results
nodes.notes: Important: BG opposes false renunciation. Track MBH sages who address this

nodes.name: Yoga as Equanimity in Action
nodes.proposition: Yoga is skill in action; perform actions as yoga, established in equanimity
nodes.notes: Track how yoga is defined vs how it is used colloquially across MBH

nodes.name: Desire as Root of All Bondage
nodes.proposition: Desire (kama) is the enemy — from desire comes anger, from anger delusion, from delusion destruction of discrimination
nodes.notes: The chain kama-krodha-moha-nasht is a complete causal sequence. Track it in MBH.

nodes.name: Duty According to One's Nature — Svadharma
nodes.proposition: It is better to do one's own dharma imperfectly than another's dharma well
nodes.notes: Svadharma is not just caste-duty — it is action aligned with one's nature

nodes.name: Knowledge Destroys Ignorance
nodes.proposition: As fire burns fuel, knowledge burns all karmas; there is nothing purifying like knowledge
nodes.notes: Jnana as fire — track this metaphor across MBH

nodes.name: Brahman as the Origin and End of All
nodes.proposition: From Brahman beings arise, in Brahman they exist, into Brahman they return
nodes.notes: Cosmological frame — all things from one source and return to it

nodes.name: The Imperishable Beyond All Dualities
nodes.proposition: That which is beyond the two perishable and imperishable, called Purushottama — that is the highest
nodes.notes: The transcendent beyond even the avyakta — track this highest designation

nodes.name: Surrender — Sarva Dharman Parityajya
nodes.proposition: Abandon all dharmas and take refuge in Me alone — I will liberate you from all sins
nodes.notes: BG's culminating statement — track zaraNam across MBH as ultimate refuge

nodes.name: Devotion — Bhakti as Highest Path
nodes.proposition: By undivided devotion alone can I be known, seen, and entered into
nodes.notes: Track bhakti language — not worship language but the one-pointed love/attention

nodes.name: The Wise Grieve for Neither Living nor Dead
nodes.proposition: The learned grieve neither for those who have died nor for those still living
nodes.notes: BG opens with this rebuke of Arjuna's grief. Track this pattern — wise person not grieving — across MBH

nodes.name: Non-Attachment to Fruits — Phala Phalatyaga
nodes.proposition: Having performed a sacrificial action, relinquish the fruit — this is the teaching of the renouncer
nodes.notes: Non-I-ness and non-mine-ness as twin props of liberation

nodes.name: Silence and Self-Control — Maunam Indriyanigraha
nodes.proposition: Control of speech, body, and mind — austerity of the highest order
nodes.notes: Mauna as spiritual practice — track its occurrence in sage descriptions

nodes.name: Impermanence of the World — Duhkhalaya
nodes.proposition: This world is a home of suffering and impermanence — do not take refuge in it
nodes.notes: Impermanence — shared with Buddhist framework but here in Vedantic register

nodes.name: The Cosmic Person — Vishwarupa
nodes.proposition: All of existence is a manifestation of the cosmic form — the entire world is sustained in a fragment of the divine
nodes.notes: Cosmological vision — track vibhuti language across MBH

nodes.name: Time as the Destroyer — Kala
nodes.proposition: I am Time, the great destroyer of worlds — even without you these warriors shall not exist
nodes.notes: Kala as cosmic principle — absolutely central to MBH's own self-understanding

nodes.name: Liberation — Moksha as the Highest Goal
nodes.proposition: Having attained Me, the great-souled ones do not return to rebirth — they have attained the highest perfection
nodes.notes: Moksha as non-return — the telos — track across all MBH discourse

nodes.name: Friend of All Beings — Sarvabhutahite Ratah
nodes.proposition: The sage who is friend to all beings, compassionate, free of possessiveness — such a devotee is dear to Me
nodes.notes: The ethical face of ekam sat — because all are one, harm to any is harm to self

nodes.name: The Witness Self — Sakshi Chetana
nodes.proposition: The supreme self is the witness, the permitter, the upholder, the experiencer, the great lord
nodes.notes: The sakshi concept — pure witness consciousness — track in MBH sage descriptions

nodes.name: True Sacrifice — Yajna as Inner Fire
nodes.proposition: The highest yajna is the sacrifice of self into the fire of Brahman — not ritual but knowledge
nodes.notes: Internalization of yajna — ritual becomes knowledge. Major thread.

nodes.name: He Who Sees Inaction in Action
nodes.proposition: One who sees inaction in action and action in inaction is wise among humans — liberated while acting
nodes.notes: The paradox of liberated action — among the most subtle BG teachings

nodes.name: The Divine and Demonic Natures
nodes.proposition: Two classes of beings: those with divine nature (fearlessness, purity, compassion) and demonic (deceit, arrogance, cruelty)
nodes.notes: The moral typology — track these character descriptions across MBH narrative

nodes.name: Ishvara as Inner Ruler of All Hearts
nodes.proposition: The Lord dwells in the heart of all beings, causing them to revolve by maya as if mounted on a machine
nodes.notes: The inner ruler — Ishvara as indwelling presence, not external deity

nodes.name: Practice and Dispassion — Abhyasa Vairagya
nodes.proposition: By practice and dispassion the mind is restrained — these two are the means
nodes.notes: The practical method — how does one actually do this? Track across MBH

nodes.name: Purification of Mind — Chitta Shuddhi
nodes.proposition: With a purified intellect, controlling the self with fortitude, having abandoned sense objects, free of passion and aversion
nodes.notes: Purity as prerequisite to realization — track in descriptions of qualified sages

nodes.name: Not the Doer — Ahamkara as False Agent
nodes.proposition: One who is deluded by ego thinks 'I am the doer' — but it is the gunas that act
nodes.notes: Non-doership — the agent-self is the root illusion

nodes.name: Brahmavidya — Knowledge of Brahman as Supreme
nodes.proposition: This is the royal knowledge, royal secret, supremely purifying, directly known, in accord with dharma
nodes.notes: The meta-node: BG itself claims to be Brahmavidya. Track this self-designation.
